architecture_summary: Yi uses a modified decoder-only Transformer architecture based on LLaMA’s implementation, with Grouped-Query Attention (GQA), SwiGLU, and RoPE with an adjusted base frequency (RoPE ABF). The paper extends the base 6B and 34B language models into chat models, 200K long-context models, depth-upscaled models, and vision-language models.

datasets_mentioned.surface: 3.1 trillion tokens of English and Chinese corpora

Performance between Yi-6B and Yi-9B: Arc Challenge (25-shot), HellaSwag (10-shot) MMLU (5-shot), Winogrande (5-shot), GSM8K (5-shot), MATH (4-shot), HumanEval pass@1, MBPP pass@1(3-shot). Yi-9B Init is just depthwise upscaling from Yi-6B by duplicating layers 12-28 without further training.

novel_contributions: A series of language and multimodal models based on 6B and 34B pretrained language models, extended to chat models, 200K long context models, depth-upscaled models, and vision-language models.
A cascaded data deduplication and quality filtering pipeline for constructing 3.1 trillion tokens of English and Chinese corpora.
A small scale (less than 10K) instruction dataset polished over multiple iterations such that every single instance has been verified directly by machine learning engineers.
A lightweight continual pretraining approach to extend context length to 200K and demonstrate strong needle-in-a-haystack retrieval performance.
A depth-upscaling approach that expands Yi-6B into Yi-9B by duplicating middle layers and continuing pretraining.
A three-stage vision-language training method built on Yi-6B-Chat and Yi-34B-Chat with a CLIP ViT-H/14 initialized vision encoder.

key_claims.claim: The Yi model family is based on 6B and 34B pretrained language models and is extended to chat models, 200K long context models, depth-upscaled models, and vision-language models.

Yi attributes its performance primarily to data quality from data-engineering efforts, including 3.1 trillion tokens of English and Chinese corpora for pretraining and a small, manually verified instruction dataset for finetuning.
Yi-34B generally achieves scores on par with GPT-3.5 on benchmarks like MMLU and LMSys ELO Rating.
The base model uses a modified decoder-only Transformer with GQA, SwiGLU, and RoPE ABF.
The finetuning stage uses less than 10K instruction-response pairs, with next-word prediction loss computed only on responses.
The paper extends context length to 200K through lightweight continual pretraining and reports strong needle-in-a-haystack retrieval performance.
Yi-VL uses a ViT initialized with CLIP ViT-H/14, a two-layer MLP projection module, and a Yi-Chat language model in a three-stage training process.
Depth upscaling from Yi-6B to Yi-9B by duplicating layers 12-28 improves performance across Arc-C, HellaSwag, MMLU, Winogrande, GSM8K, MATH, HumanEval, and MBPP.
